div1.entryFree: Severe, rigid, hard, merciless: the boar, that bloody beast, which knows no pity, but is still s. Ven. 1000 (rhyming). it shall be merciful and too s. Ven. 1000 Lord Angelo is s. Meas. II, 1, 296. O just but s. law, II, 2, 41. III, 2, 267. he who the sword of heaven will bear should be as holy as s. III, 2, 267 with eyes s. and beard of formal cut, As II, 7, 155. such strict and s. covenants, H6A V, 4, 114. the rigour of --st law, Rom. V, 3, 269. you are too s. a moraler, Oth. II, 3, 301.
